metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_273
metadata.comment: Rationale: Littleton et al used a Drosophila temperature-sensitive paralytic mutation in NSF (termed "comatose") that slowly blocks synaptic transmission while syntaxin and SNARE complexes accumulate on synaptic vesicles. Figure 4 shows that increased temperature leads to accumulation of 70S SNARE assembled SNARE complex. These findings suggest that NSF mediates disassembly of the SNARE complex after fusion, and that disassembly is required for the re-generation of the SNARE-fusion machinery.. Experimental description: Flies were cultured on standard medium at 23°C. syx3-69 was generated in an F2 EMS screen for paralytic mutations on the third chromosome (marked with st) and mapped to 3-81 by recombination with Gl Sb H and Pr Dr chromosomes.
